title: Salt of the Earth, Light of the World
subtitle: On the mountain, Christ tells His own they must shine in deed and word — and that He came not to break the Law but to fulfill it

stillness: First to live well, then to teach well.
Let the light within you be kindled before you speak.
gospel: You are the salt of the earth.
But if the salt loses its savor, with what shall it be salted?
It is good for nothing any more but to be cast out,
and to be trodden on by men.
You are the light of the world.
A city seated on a mountain cannot be hid.
Neither do men light a candle and put it under a bushel,
but upon a candlestick, that it may shine to all that are in the house.
So let your light shine before men,
that they may see your good works,
and glorify your Father who is in heaven.
Do not think that I am come to destroy the Law;
I am not come to destroy, but to fulfill.
behold: Attend to each thing as if you were present.
Behold the Lord upon the mountain, the Apostles gathered close,
and hear Him liken them to four things: salt, light, a city, a lamp.
He had urged them to endure tribulation;
now He shows them what they are — what they must be.
Behold the salt: it makes the barren earth fruitful,
it seasons food, it dries flesh, it keeps it from rotting;
it is made of water and fire, and offered in every sacrifice.
Behold the lamp set high upon its stand,
the city upon the mountain that cannot be hidden —
and see yourself there, called to shine to all in the house.
consider: You are the salt of the earth — that is, you must be.
By the example of holiness the earthly affection is made barren,
holy desire is given savor, the flesh is dried by mortification,
and the rot of lust is held back and kept from spreading.
This salt is made from the water of devotion and the fire of love,
boiled down in the furnace of penance.
But if the salt grows foolish and fades — through fear of persecution,
through love of prosperity, through the swelling of vain glory,
through the pull of carnal affection, through the sleep of negligence —
then with what shall the weak people under him be salted?
Who will heal the snake-charmer, once the serpent has struck him?
To such a one it must be said: Physician, heal yourself.
Salt that has lost its savor is good for nothing,
neither for the earth nor for the dunghill, but to be cast out and trodden underfoot —
trodden by men through mockery, by angels in judgment through separation,
by demons in hell through affliction.
You are also the light of the world.
First to live well, then to teach well: salt in example, light in doctrine.
God is the light not illumined but illumining;
the apostolic men are light both illumined and illumining;
the simple just are light illumined but not illumining —
as the light of the sun, of the moon, and of the star.
A city on a mountain cannot be hidden, nor a lamp set under a bushel.
So hide not yourself, nor the lamp of the divine word
beneath the bushel of human fear, nor beneath the bed of worldly ease.
Be a city on the mountain, a refuge for the unjustly oppressed.
He did not say: that men hearing your good word —
but: that men seeing your good works may glorify your Father.
For one profits more by doing than by speaking,
and examples move more than words, for the splendor of deed is greater than of speech.
The one preaches only with his mouth, one hour in the week;
the other preaches wholly, in every hour of all his time.
A loud-speaking tongue and an idle hand,
a shining doctrine and a darkened life — this is a monstrous thing.
Seek not your own glory in this, but God's, and the building up of your neighbor.
Then He begins: I came not to destroy the Law, but to fulfill it.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, who to the men of old promised the things of time,
but to us things eternal, that our justice may overflow:
grant me to shine before You and before my neighbors in word and in deed,
and not to break Your Law, but to fulfill it beyond measure.
Keep me from anger and from offending my neighbor,
that the gift of heart, of mouth, or of good work which I offer You
may be acceptable in Your sight.
Grant me, most merciful God, to guard against evil desires and glances,
to turn away from every oath,
that as I abstain from wronging my neighbor, so also from wronging You,
and may please You in all things, always. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Magis enim operando quis proficit quam loquendo, et plus movent exempla quam verba, quia major est splendor operis quam sermonis.
latinJewel.english: For one profits more by doing than by speaking, and examples move more than words, because the splendor of a deed is greater than that of speech.
